_about: Wire frames extracted VERBATIM from the upstream OpenClaw unit tests at tag v2026.7.1 (github.com/openclaw/openclaw), adapted only for sessionKey/runId namespacing. Each scenario pins one interpretation contract documented in docs/design/upstream-interpretation-comparison.md — the shapes the gateway's own test suite guarantees on the wire. Source test file:line is cited per scenario. Replayed by test/upstream-frames.test.ts; do not edit shapes without re-checking the upstream tag.

aborted-user-stop-partial-text.description: src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts:335-346 — a killed run broadcasts state:aborted with a free-form stopReason ('user' is a caller value, NOT in any enum) and the partial text snapshot. The turn must finalize aborted on state alone; stopReason must never reclassify it.

aborted-tool-validation-errormessage.description: src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts:2954-2961 — self-abort on tool-validation failure: state:aborted carries stopReason:'aborted' plus errorMessage (the ONLY errorMessage the gateway ever puts on an aborted frame, filtered by readToolValidationErrorSummary). Atrium finalizes aborted and deliberately does not persist that errorMessage as a message error.

payload.errorMessage: edit tool validation failed: edits: must be an array

error-timeout-stopreason-precedence.description: src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts:3023-3043 — a timed-out abort (lifecycle end aborted:true stopReason:'timeout') is emitted as state:ERROR, not aborted: the gateway consumes stopReason BEFORE emission and `state` carries the decision. The bridge must classify on state alone (error), never re-derive from stopReason.

error-errorkind-rate-limit.description: src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts:3829-3865 — a 429 lifecycle error reaches the wire as state:error with errorKind:'rate_limit' (detectErrorKind regex on the gateway side). The allowlisted wire kind must survive as the message's stable errorCode.

payload.errorMessage: Provider call failed: 429 Too many requests

content.text: Error: Provider call failed: 429 Too many requests

embedded-takeover-after-content.description: src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts:1147-1152 (message thrown verbatim in failover-error.test.ts:1267-1273) — the session-file takeover error after real streamed content. Upstream refuses any retry once there is send evidence (subagent-announce-delivery.ts:383-397); the bridge's homologue is the hasRealContent() downgrade: close COMPLETE, class on the trace-only channel.

payload.errorMessage: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released: /tmp/openclaw/session.jsonl

init-conflict-zero-content.description: src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts (message verbatim in extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts:2610) — the pre-generation OCC init conflict. Upstream channels retry it with backoff; the bridge classifies it to the stable session_init_conflict code that Convex's bounded auto-retry keys on. Zero content is possible here (the error is thrown before any model call).

compaction-explicit-stream-signals.description: src/infra/agent-events.test.ts:460-464 + src/plugins/wired-hooks-compaction.test.ts:217-222 — the EXPLICIT mid-turn compaction signal: {stream:'compaction', phase:'start'} then {phase:'end', willRetry:true, completed:true}, with NO lifecycle end (the run pauses and continues on the same runId). The bridge CONSUMES these as the primary mid-turn compaction signal (one persisted 'midturn' marker, widened silence budget until content resumes) and must NEVER reset the accumulated text; the follow-on deltas append and the turn completes intact.
